profile.name: Prof. Hakan Kayal
roles: Professor and Chair, Space Technology, University of Wurzburg (JMU)
Founder, IFEX (Interdisciplinary Research Centre for Extraterrestrial Studies)
Principal Investigator, SkyCAM UAP Ground Observatory
profile.summary: German space technology professor who founded IFEX - the first European academic institution to formally incorporate UAP into its research mandate - and deployed SONATE-2, a 6U CubeSat currently in orbit carrying onboard AI specifically trained to detect and classify anomalous objects. His SkyCAM AI camera is operational at the University of Wurzburg campus and his team participates in the DLR-funded VaMEx Mars mission with a camera tasked to search for anomalous phenomena in the Martian atmosphere.
education: Professor, Department of Computer Science, Julius-Maximilians-Universitat Wurzburg
early_career: Developed autonomous onboard processing and fault detection systems for spacecraft and nanosatellites
Established the Space Technology chair at JMU Wurzburg, building expertise in small satellite design, onboard AI, and anomaly detection for space systems

key_events.event: Founded IFEX (Interdisciplinary Research Centre for Extraterrestrial Studies) at University of Wurzburg in January 2022. IFEX became the first academic institution in Germany and Europe to formally include UAP in its research agenda alongside astrobiology and SETI.

Deployed SkyCAM, an AI-powered ground-based camera system at the Hubland Campus of the University of Wurzburg, specifically designed to detect and analyze anomalous aerial phenomena using real-time machine learning classification.

IFEX expanded its affiliate network to include international researchers. Kevin Knuth (University at Albany) and Matt Szydagis joined as associate members, connecting IFEX to the UAPx field research program and establishing a transatlantic UAP research collaboration.

Launched SONATE-2, a 6U CubeSat, aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket. SONATE-2 carries multiple cameras and onboard AI trained to autonomously recognize and classify anomalous objects in Earth orbit. It is the first academic satellite with an explicit UAP anomaly detection mission currently in orbit.

Presented early SONATE-2 in-flight results at the 4S Symposium (Small Satellites, Systems and Services) in Palma de Mallorca. Published 'Early Results and In-Flight Experience of the 6U-Mission SONATE-2' reporting the satellite's autonomous anomaly classification performance.

Participated in the VaMEx (Valles Marineris Explorer) German Mars mission design, contributing a camera specifically tasked with searching for anomalous phenomena in the Martian atmosphere. This is the first DLR-funded research project to include an explicit UAP detection component on another planet.

Co-authored 'The New Science of Unidentified Aerospace-Undersea Phenomena (UAP)' in Progress in Aerospace Sciences (arXiv:2502.06794) with Kevin Knuth (lead), Matt Szydagis, Beatriz Villarroel, and 30+ other researchers. Published 'Novel onboard data processing strategies on nanosatellite SONATE-2' in SPIE Proceedings, reporting advanced onboard AI classification results.

associated_people.name: Dr. Kevin Knuth
associated_people.role: Professor of Physics, UAPx Lead Scientist
associated_people.relationship: Co-authors on the 2025 'New Science of UAP' flagship paper. Knuth is an IFEX affiliate member, directly connecting the UAPx field research program to Kayal's satellite and ground-based detection infrastructure. Together they represent the hardware and theory pillars of the same collaborative UAP research network.

associated_people.name: Dr. Matt Szydagis
associated_people.role: Assistant Professor of Physics, UAPx Field Research Leader
associated_people.relationship: Co-authors on the 2025 'New Science of UAP' paper. Szydagis is an IFEX affiliate member. Both focus on instrumented detection - Szydagis on ground field expeditions, Kayal on orbital and ground-based observatories.

associated_people.name: Dr. Beatriz Villarroel
associated_people.relationship: Co-authors on the 2025 'New Science of UAP' flagship paper. Villarroel's retrospective archival plate analysis and Kayal's real-time orbital detection represent complementary temporal approaches - historical photographic records versus live satellite monitoring.

claims.claim: SONATE-2, a 6U CubeSat in low Earth orbit, can autonomously detect and classify anomalous objects using onboard AI trained on known satellite and debris signatures, providing a scalable model for academic orbital UAP monitoring.

SkyCAM, an AI-powered ground observatory deployed at the University of Wurzburg, can systematically monitor the sky for anomalous aerial phenomena using machine learning classification without continuous human review.
A camera deployed on the DLR-funded VaMEx Mars mission, explicitly tasked with anomalous phenomenon detection, represents the first government-funded extraterrestrial UAP search component.

career_connections.relationship: Knuth is an IFEX affiliate member; co-authors on 2025 New Science of UAP paper.
Szydagis is an IFEX affiliate member; co-authors on 2025 New Science of UAP paper.
Co-authors on 2025 New Science of UAP flagship paper; complementary temporal detection approaches.
Founder and director of the Interdisciplinary Research Centre for Extraterrestrial Studies at JMU Wurzburg.
